title: lepechinia inflata
intro: Lepechinia inflata, also known as Mexican mountain mint, is a perennial plant native to the highlands of Mexico and Central America. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The plant is valued for its health benefits, such as treating respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Lepechinia inflata can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support the immune system and promote overall well-being.
